extension.valueMarkdown:
## Character
51-year-old Hispanic male, high school shop teacher and baseball coach. Lives in San Antonio, TX with his wife Rosa and youngest daughter (age 16). Born in San Antonio, lifelong Texan. Insurance: employer plan through the school district (Aetna). Active lifestyle compromised by progressive joint disease.

## Clinical Scenario
Carlos was diagnosed with seropositive rheumatoid arthritis in 2021 after 6 months of progressive symmetric polyarthritis (hands, wrists, feet). His PCP noted elevated inflammatory markers and referred him to a rheumatologist. Initial workup showed positive RF (78 IU/mL), positive anti-CCP (>250 U/mL), elevated ESR (42) and CRP (2.8), and erosive changes on hand X-rays.

He was started on methotrexate 15mg weekly with folic acid. After 4 months of adequate dosing, his DAS28-CRP remained elevated at 4.2 (moderate disease activity). Methotrexate was increased to 20mg weekly. At 6 months on 20mg, he developed elevated liver enzymes (ALT 98, AST 82) — methotrexate was discontinued for hepatotoxicity.

He was switched to leflunomide 20mg daily. After 6 months on leflunomide, his DAS28-CRP was 4.8 (moderate-high), with worsening hand grip strength and new wrist erosions on follow-up imaging. Leflunomide was deemed an inadequate response.

His rheumatologist recommends initiating adalimumab (Humira biosimilar — Hadlima) as step-up biologic therapy. The prior authorization requires documentation of: RA diagnosis, failed conventional DMARDs (methotrexate — hepatotoxicity; leflunomide — inadequate response), disease activity scores, functional status, and imaging showing progression.

Aetna uses a Permission Ticket to access Carlos's rheumatology and PCP records to adjudicate the prior authorization request.

## Sites
- **Alamo Family Practice** — PCP in San Antonio, TX (2015–present). General primary care, initial joint complaint workup, referral to rheumatology, routine management.
- **South Texas Rheumatology** — Rheumatologist in San Antonio, TX (2021–present). RA diagnosis, DMARD management, disease activity monitoring, biologic prior auth documentation.

Two sites, one state (TX). Tests Permission Ticket constraints on **scopes** (payer needs Condition, Observation [disease activity scores, labs], DiagnosticReport [imaging], MedicationRequest [DMARD history], Procedure — focused on RA treatment) and **periods** (payer needs the full DMARD treatment history from 2021–present).

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily as needed for seasonal allergy symptoms
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily as needed for seasonal allergy symptoms

reasonReference.display: Symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis, suspected rheumatoid arthritis

dosageInstruction.text: Take by mouth as needed for joint pain and stiffness, with food. Avoid prolonged daily use without gastroprotection.
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take by mouth as needed for joint pain and stiffness, with food. Avoid prolonged daily use without gastroprotection.

note.text: BMI 28.8 at annual wellness 2022-10-17. Weight trending up (201 lbs), likely related to decreased physical activity secondary to RA functional limitations. Counseled on dietary modifications and low-impact exercise.
Occasional low back pain after long days in the shop, managed with stretching. No red flag symptoms. Likely occupational.
Worsening lipid profile at 2022-10-17: LDL 132 mg/dL, HDL 42 mg/dL, TG 188 mg/dL. 10-year ASCVD risk 6.8%. Lifestyle modifications discussed; statin deferred pending further risk stratification.

code.text: Morning finger stiffness, bilateral, new onset

note.text: Bilateral morning finger stiffness lasting 15-20 minutes, resolving with activity. No objective joint swelling. Attributed by patient to occupational use and cooler weather. Plan to monitor; return for in-office evaluation if duration exceeds 30 minutes, worsens, or swelling develops.

code.text: Symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis, suspected rheumatoid arthritis

note.text: Symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis involving bilateral MCPs, PIPs, wrists, and MTPs with morning stiffness >1 hour. Strongly positive RF (78 IU/mL) and anti-CCP (>250 U/mL) with elevated inflammatory markers (ESR 42, CRP 2.8). High clinical suspicion for rheumatoid arthritis. Urgent referral to rheumatology placed.

New diagnosis. A1c 5.7% (prediabetes range 5.7-6.4%), fasting glucose 104 mg/dL. Counseled on dietary modifications and increased physical activity as tolerated given RA limitations. Will monitor A1c in 6 months.
Tdap booster administered; last Td was >10 years prior. Patient tolerated well, observed 15 minutes post-administration without adverse reaction.

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos presented as a new patient to Alamo Family Practice after his previous clinic closed. Dr.

reasonCode.text: Annual wellness examination, Tdap booster immunization

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos presented for his annual wellness examination. He reported feeling well overall; occasional low back pain after long days in the shop but manageable with stretching.

Carlos connected via video visit for his annual wellness during the COVID-19 pandemic. He reported being generally well but mentioned increasing morning stiffness in his fingers over the past few weeks, which he attributed to cooler weather and gripping tools in the shop.

reasonCode.text: Joint pain and stiffness evaluation, labs ordered

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos presented with a chief complaint of worsening bilateral hand stiffness and pain over the past 3–4 months. He reported that morning stiffness now lasted over an hour and was affecting his ability to make a full fist, demonstrate lathe techniques in the shop, and grip a bat during early-season baseball practice.

reasonCode.text: Lab results review, rheumatology referral confirmation

extension.valueMarkdown: Dr. Tran called Carlos to review the lab results in detail and confirm the rheumatology referral.

coding.display: Administration of vaccine to produce active immunity (procedure)

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos presented for his first dose of the Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine, now available to his age group in Texas. He reported that he had started methotrexate under Dr.
Carlos presented for routine lab draw as part of coordinated DMARD monitoring. Dr.

coding.display: History and physical examination, annual for health maintenance (procedure)

reasonCode.text: Annual wellness examination with rheumatoid arthritis co-management review

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos presented for his annual wellness examination. He reported ongoing joint stiffness and pain, currently on leflunomide under Dr.

reasonCode.text: Annual wellness examination and medication review

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos connected via video visit for his annual wellness. He reported that the biologic prior authorization process with Aetna was ongoing and expressed frustration with the delays.

code.text: Urgent referral to rheumatology — South Texas Rheumatology, Dr. Alejandro Fuentes

reasonReference.display: Symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis, suspected rheumatoid arthritis

note.text: Urgent referral to South Texas Rheumatology, Dr. Alejandro Fuentes, for diagnostic confirmation and DMARD initiation. Target consultation within 4-6 weeks. Referral documentation includes lab results (RF 78, anti-CCP >250, ESR 42, CRP 2.8) and clinical exam findings.
